---
title: "DESeq2 multiple conditions"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*.

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.

# Initial setup

```{r Rmd setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "/project/def-mouellet/Scripts_MOU/PNP/alliancecan/deseq2/")
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r Set working directory}
# Set working directory
setwd("/project/def-mouellet/Scripts_MOU/PNP/alliancecan/deseq2/")
getwd()
```

```{r Activate renv}
# Activate environment
renv::activate()
```


```{r Load libraries}
# Load required packages
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Set seed
set.seed(42)
```





# Load data

```{r Count data}
# Count data
data <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/input/Mov10_full_counts.txt", 
                   header = T, row.names = 1)
str(data)
```

```{r Col data}
# Col data / Sample info / Metadata
meta <- read.table("example_multiple_conditions/input/Mov10_full_meta.txt", 
                   header = T, row.names = 1)

# Convert to factor
meta[] <- lapply(meta, as.factor) # all columns
str(meta)
```

```{r Sample info}
print(meta)
```

```{r Check sample name match}
# Check that sample names match in both files
## Composition check
all(colnames(data) %in% rownames(meta)) # Should be TRUE
## Order check
all(colnames(data) == rownames(meta))
## If order check gives FALSE
data_raw <- data
data <- data_raw[, match(rownames(meta), colnames(data_raw))]
all(colnames(data) == rownames(meta))
```





# QC

## Count distribution

```{r}
# Long format
data_long <- data %>%
  gather(
    colnames(data),
    key = "sample_name",
    value = "raw_counts"
  )
```

```{r}
# Count distribution
ggplot(
  data_long,
  aes(x = raw_counts)
  ) +
  geom_histogram(stat = "bin", bins = 100) +
  facet_wrap(~ sample_name) +
  xlab("Raw expression counts") +
  ylab("Number of genes") +
  theme_bw()
```

A low number of counts associated with a large proportion of genes



## Count data modeling

Poisson: mean == variance

Binomial: mean \< variance

```{r}
# MOV10_overexpression replicates
mean_counts <- apply(data[, c("Mov10_oe_1", "Mov10_oe_2", "Mov10_oe_3")], 1, mean)
var_counts <- apply(data[, c("Mov10_oe_1", "Mov10_oe_2", "Mov10_oe_3")], 1, var)
df <- data.frame(mean_counts, var_counts)

ggplot(df) +
  geom_point(aes(x = mean_counts, y = var_counts)) +
  geom_line(aes(x = mean_counts, y = mean_counts, color="red"), show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  theme_classic()
```



## Normalized counts

```{r Create DESeqDataSet object}
# Create DESeqDataSet object
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = data,
  colData = meta,
  design = ~sampletype
)
```

```{r Original count matrix}
# The original count matrix
head(counts(dds))
```

```{r DESeq2 normalization}
# DESeq2 normalization: median of ratios
dds <- estimateSizeFactors(dds)
sizeFactors(dds)
```

```{r Normalized counts matrix}
# Normalized counts matrix
normalized_counts <- counts(dds, normalized = TRUE)
write.table(normalized_counts, 
           file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts.tsv", 
           sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```



## Normalized counts transformed
Only necessary for visualization in QC

### rlog transformation
rlog() works well on small datasets (n < 30)

```{r rlog()}
rld <- rlog(dds, blind = TRUE)
rld_mat <- assay(rld)
write.table(rld_mat, 
           file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_rlog.tsv", 
           sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```

### vst transformation
vst() is faster than rlog(), recommended for medium to large datasets (n > 30)

```{r vst()}
vsd <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE) # n genes >= 1000
# vsd <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds, blind = TRUE) # n genes < 1000
vsd_mat <- assay(vsd)
write.table(vsd_mat, 
           file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_vst.tsv", 
           sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```

### log2(n+1) transformation

```{r log2(n+1)}
ntd <- normTransform(dds)
ntd_mat <- assay(ntd)
write.table(ntd_mat, 
            file = "example_multiple_conditions/output/normalized_counts_log2.tsv", 
            sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
```



## PCA
This example use rlog transformation. vst or log2(n+1) could be used similarly

### PC1 & PC2

```{r plotPCA()}
plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "sampletype") # Top 500 most variable genes
# plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "sampletype", ntop = 100) # Top 100 most variable genes
```

```{r PCA data}
pcaData <- plotPCA(rld, intgroup = c("sampletype", "MOVexpr"), returnData = TRUE)
pcaData
```

```{r percentVar}
# Get the percentage variation
attributes(pcaData)
percentVar <- round(100 * attr(pcaData, "percentVar"))
```

```{r PCA ggplot2}
# Plot PCA using ggplot2
ggplot(
  pcaData,
  aes(
    x = PC1, y = PC2,
    color = sampletype, 
    shape = MOVexpr
    )
) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC1 (", percentVar[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", percentVar[2], "%)")
    ) +
    theme_classic()

ggsave("example_multiple_conditions/output/PCA.pdf", width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

### Additional PCs

```{r PCs data}
pca <- prcomp(t(rld_mat)) # all genes
attributes(pca)
```

```{r PCs dataframe}
as.data.frame(pca$x)
```

```{r PCs dataframe with metadata}
pca_w_meta_df <- merge(
  as.data.frame(pca$x), 
  meta, 
  by = 0, # row names as merging key
  all = TRUE # full join
  )
pca_w_meta_df
```

```{r Percentage variation}
# Percentage variation
percentVar <- round((pca$sdev^2) / sum(pca$sdev^2) * 100, 1)
percentVar
```


```{r PC3-4 ggplot2}
ggplot(
  pca_w_meta_df,
  aes(
    x = PC3, y = PC4,
    color = sampletype, 
    shape = MOVexpr
    )
) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC3 (", percentVar[3], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC4 (", percentVar[4], "%)")
    ) +
  theme_classic()
```
